about: Lightweight directional evals for the SynthCheck method. Each scenario has a KNOWN correct ranking; a faithful run should reproduce the ordering (absolute numbers will vary — that's expected, output is directional). Use these to verify the method ranks correctly and doesn't collapse to uniform scores.
pass_criteria: Correct RANK order on the checked dimension + visibly different distributions across cells (no variance collapse) + at least one real objection surfaced.

scenarios.stimulus: A simple habit-tracking mobile app, solo-built, no standout feature vs free alternatives.
scenarios.expected_ranking: $99/mo should rank lowest on purchase intent; intent should fall monotonically as price rises for the budget cohort; the premium cohort may flatten or invert at the low end (price-as-quality-signal).
checks: no cell has ~identical distribution to another

scenarios.stimulus: Headline A: 'The best way to manage your finances.' vs Headline B: 'See every subscription you forgot you're paying for — in 30 seconds.'
scenarios.expected_ranking: Headline B (specific, concrete benefit) should beat Headline A (vague) on comprehension, scroll-stop, and CTA-click intent across both cohorts.
checks: B > A on at least 2 of 3 metrics
skeptic cohort surfaces a believability objection on B

scenarios.stimulus: Free personalized assessment — results unlock a call with our team.
scenarios.expected_ranking: The skeptic/red-team cohort must surface the 'this is a sales funnel / high-ticket upsell trap' objection; motivated cohort should show higher top-2-box than the red-team.
checks: red-team objection names the upsell/sales-call risk
a validate-next real-money test is proposed
